title: eriophorum polystachion subsp. congestum
intro: Eriophorum polystachion subsp. congestum, also known as the cotton grass, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the Eriophorum genus and is native to parts of Europe and Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its use in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and congestion due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Eriophorum polystachion subsp. congestum include its ability to ease congestion and reduce inflammation, making it a useful remedy for people suffering from respiratory problems. Traditionally, the plant is prepared for medicinal use by infusing the roots and rhizomes in hot water to create a herbal tea or by using the dried roots and rhizomes in a decoction, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
